Marcus Wise Engine · Blank
250 cards

Lists · Candlelight

100 cards · 9 BG presets
IMP-LISTS-01
Ember

Eight Signs You Are Being Slowly Devalued

  1. 1.Compliments that used to arrive unprompted now require a performance first.
  2. 2.Casual put-downs appear inside jokes, with practiced deniability.
  3. 3.Your wins get minimized with sentences that sound generous.
  4. 4.Affection becomes conditional on your compliance in tiny ways.
  5. 5.Your stories get interrupted in ways they never used to be.
  6. 6.Plans you set become negotiable the moment they cost the other person.
  7. 7.You feel a low-grade embarrassment for needs that used to be welcomed.
  8. 8.The relationship temperature drops by one degree every season, with no announcement.
The devaluation is patient. The recognition is sudden.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-02
Midnight

Nine Phrases Narcissists Use To Test Your Limits

  1. 1.I was just being honest — after delivering a cruelty.
  2. 2.You used to be more fun — controlling through nostalgia.
  3. 3.Everyone says it, I just have the guts — manufacturing a fake consensus.
  4. 4.I would never have said it if it were not true — license to deliver anything.
  5. 5.That is your interpretation — denying impact without denying the act.
  6. 6.I am not going to walk on eggshells — refusing accountability as character.
  7. 7.You always make this about you — when you bring up your own need.
  8. 8.I am the only one who tells you the truth — isolating you from other input.
  9. 9.This is who I am — closing the door on change while leaving it open for damage.
The test is the relationship. The relationship is the test.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-03
Tobacco

Ten Ways Covert Manipulators Isolate You

  1. 1.They schedule events with you that conflict with your friends.
  2. 2.They find fault with each person you bring around them.
  3. 3.They become cold the day after you see your family.
  4. 4.They complain gently that your friends do not include them.
  5. 5.They speed the move-in, the relocation, the joint account.
  6. 6.They interpret your need for solitude as rejection of them.
  7. 7.They narrate your relationships to you, identifying who is bad for you.
  8. 8.They withhold approval of plans that do not include them.
  9. 9.They create tension you have to mediate at every social event.
  10. 10.They become the gatekeeper of contact with your own family.
Isolation rarely arrives announced. It arrives logistical.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-04
Candle

Eight Signs The Relationship Reset Will Not Stick

  1. 1.The apology arrived without naming the specific harm.
  2. 2.The promise to change came with a list of conditions on you.
  3. 3.The same pattern resurfaced within three weeks under a different surface.
  4. 4.The reset arrived only when they sensed you would actually leave.
  5. 5.Their behavior softened but their structure of relating did not.
  6. 6.You are still managing their reactions to the topic of the harm.
  7. 7.The repair conversation happens only on their schedule, never yours.
  8. 8.You feel more careful, not less, six weeks in.
Repair without behavior change is a delay, not a return.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-05
Ash

Nine Patterns Of Intermittent Reinforcement

  1. 1.Long stretches of coldness punctuated by sudden warmth.
  2. 2.Affection that arrives right after the worst incidents.
  3. 3.Their best behavior shows up the week you considered leaving.
  4. 4.Apologies that land hardest when delivered after a long silence.
  5. 5.You feel most loved during the recovery from being hurt.
  6. 6.The relationship oscillates between peak and crisis, no middle.
  7. 7.Small generosities arrive on the day you raise a concern.
  8. 8.The rewards are unscheduled, but the punishments are predictable.
  9. 9.You catch yourself grateful for treatment you would not accept from a stranger.
Unpredictable rewards build the strongest attachments. Often to harm.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-06
Dusk

Eight Signs Your Manager Is Setting You Up To Fail

  1. 1.Your responsibilities keep expanding without authority expanding with them.
  2. 2.You are briefed verbally on critical changes, never in writing.
  3. 3.Decisions made about your work happen in meetings you are not in.
  4. 4.You are asked to fix things that were broken before you arrived.
  5. 5.Your successes get reattributed and your failures get pinned to you.
  6. 6.Resources you need are promised repeatedly and never actually delivered.
  7. 7.Feedback arrives late, in performance reviews, never in real time.
  8. 8.Peers who used to defer to you stop, without explanation.
Documentation is the only weapon that scales.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-07
Iron

Ten Phrases To Retire From Professional Emails

  1. 1.Just circling back — say circling back.
  2. 2.Sorry to bother you — at the start of a normal request.
  3. 3.I think maybe we could — committing to nothing.
  4. 4.No worries if not — implying the worry exists.
  5. 5.Hoping to hear from you — when you mean expecting.
  6. 6.Quick question — when the question is rarely quick.
  7. 7.I am not sure if — preface that undermines what follows.
  8. 8.Please advise — the corporate phrase that says nothing.
  9. 9.Per my last email — passive-aggressive flag, even when justified.
  10. 10.Thanks in advance — pressure dressed as politeness.
The phrase shrinks the request. Drop it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-08
Velvet

Nine Things To Never Put In A Slack Message

  1. 1.Specific grievances about a named colleague.
  2. 2.Speculation about another person's salary or compensation.
  3. 3.Confidential information about an internal decision in flux.
  4. 4.Frustration with leadership decisions, even in DMs you trust.
  5. 5.Personal health details that could outlast the channel.
  6. 6.Job-search plans before you have submitted notice.
  7. 7.Complaints that are funny now but become evidence later.
  8. 8.Anything you would rephrase if your CEO joined the channel.
  9. 9.Information you do not want a hostile lawyer reading aloud.
If it would not survive a screenshot, do not type it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-09
Bone

Eight Signs Your Colleague Will Steal Credit

  1. 1.They ask probing questions about your work the week before reviews.
  2. 2.They paraphrase your ideas in meetings the next day, without attribution.
  3. 3.They volunteer to present the work to leadership on your behalf.
  4. 4.They invite themselves into projects late, after the hard part is done.
  5. 5.They suggest combining your work with theirs in a single deliverable.
  6. 6.They are warmest to you in private and most diminishing in groups.
  7. 7.Their version of the org chart always places them adjacent to your wins.
  8. 8.Their stories about their previous role consistently feature heroic solo work.
The pattern is visible before the theft. Document early.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-10
Sand

Nine Red Flags During Compensation Negotiations

  1. 1.They claim the salary band is fixed when the recruiter said otherwise.
  2. 2.They emphasize equity heavily while declining to specify the strike price.
  3. 3.They delay sending the written offer for days after verbal agreement.
  4. 4.They frame their offer as their best when it is their first.
  5. 5.They bristle visibly when you counter with a number.
  6. 6.They invoke the team's egalitarian culture to suppress your ask.
  7. 7.They promise a review in six months instead of a number now.
  8. 8.They compare you unfavorably to another candidate during the negotiation.
  9. 9.They take personal offense at standard professional pushback.
How they negotiate is how they will manage. Watch closely.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-11
Ember

Nine Signs You Were The Emotional Support Child

  1. 1.You knew the mood of the house from the sound of the front door.
  2. 2.You intervened in parental conflicts before you were old enough to drive.
  3. 3.You were praised for being mature in ways other kids your age were not.
  4. 4.You suppressed your own needs to keep the household stable.
  5. 5.Adults confided in you about things that were not your business to know.
  6. 6.You felt responsible for your parents' happiness in a load-bearing way.
  7. 7.You are uncomfortable receiving care without immediately reciprocating.
  8. 8.You feel guilty during your own crises because someone else might need attention.
  9. 9.You attract people in crisis now, and the attraction is mutual.
You learned to manage adults before you learned to manage yourself.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-12
Midnight

Eight Phrases That Reveal Generational Shame

  1. 1.We do not talk about that — about anything that produced shame two generations ago.
  2. 2.What will people think — anchoring decisions to phantom audiences.
  3. 3.That is not how we do things — refusing to name what the alternative would cost.
  4. 4.Be grateful you have it better than we did — guilt as inheritance.
  5. 5.You are bringing shame on the family — about ordinary modern choices.
  6. 6.Keep it in the family — about behavior that requires professional help.
  7. 7.That stays in this house — protecting the abuser, not the family.
  8. 8.Some things are not discussed — the boundary that protects the harm.
The shame predates the family. The phrases are how it is transmitted.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-13
Tobacco

Ten Ways Adult Siblings Still Run Childhood Scripts

  1. 1.The scapegoat still gets blamed when family logistics go wrong.
  2. 2.The golden child still receives benefit of the doubt the others do not.
  3. 3.The peacemaker still volunteers for conflicts they did not create.
  4. 4.The invisible one still has to remind everyone they exist at gatherings.
  5. 5.The responsible one still gets the crisis calls.
  6. 6.The rebel is still characterized as unstable after a decade of stability.
  7. 7.The clown is still expected to defuse rooms they did not destabilize.
  8. 8.The fixer still cannot accept help without guilt.
  9. 9.The favorite still cannot decline a request from a parent.
  10. 10.The outsider still has to translate their adult life into terms the family accepts.
The roles were assigned at age seven. They are still casting.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-14
Candle

Eight Signs You Need To Limit Family Contact

  1. 1.You feel physically tense in the hours before a family call.
  2. 2.You spend the day after recovering from the day with them.
  3. 3.You rehearse every conversation in advance to anticipate ambushes.
  4. 4.You minimize good news to protect it from being weaponized.
  5. 5.You leave each visit with a fresh grievance you cannot raise.
  6. 6.Your partner has stopped asking how the visit went.
  7. 7.You feel more like yourself the longer you are away from them.
  8. 8.Each return requires you to perform a smaller version of yourself.
Distance is not betrayal. It is calibration.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-15
Ash

Nine Things To Never Explain To Your In-Laws

  1. 1.The details of your past relationships before this one.
  2. 2.Your financial situation, current or anticipated.
  3. 3.Conflicts you are having with your partner that they will mediate.
  4. 4.Your therapy topics or mental-health history.
  5. 5.Career uncertainties you have not yet decided to share with your partner.
  6. 6.Decisions about children that are still under discussion at home.
  7. 7.Family grievances against your own parents.
  8. 8.Political views in homes where this provokes managed conflict.
  9. 9.Any complaint about your partner you would not want quoted back.
Married into the family does not mean briefed on your interior.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-16
Dusk

Nine Signs You Have Inherited Money Fear

  1. 1.You feel uneasy when you have more than three months of expenses saved.
  2. 2.You track spending obsessively even when you can afford it.
  3. 3.You feel guilty for purchases that are well within your means.
  4. 4.You underprice your work consistently and resent the result.
  5. 5.You feel suspicious when offered a deal that is structurally fair.
  6. 6.You delay asking for raises long past the appropriate window.
  7. 7.You confuse frugality with virtue in ways you would not extend to others.
  8. 8.You assume windfalls will be followed by losses, with no evidence.
  9. 9.You feel a small dread when checking your accounts even when they are healthy.
The fear predates your bank account. The bank account inherits it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-17
Iron

Eight Things To Stop Saying About Money

  1. 1.I am bad with money — past tense, future tense, or neither.
  2. 2.Money is not important — to justify decisions money is silently making for you.
  3. 3.I do not deserve to charge that much — without surveying the market.
  4. 4.People with money are different — flattening millions of humans into a class.
  5. 5.I will figure it out later — about consequences that compound monthly.
  6. 6.I cannot afford that — when you have not actually checked.
  7. 7.I would rather not talk about money — when the conversation could prevent harm.
  8. 8.Numbers stress me out — accepting illiteracy in the language of your survival.
Language about money shapes the relationship with money.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-18
Velvet

Ten Ways Your Money Mirrors Your Childhood

  1. 1.Households of scarcity produce adults who hoard, even in abundance.
  2. 2.Households of chaos produce adults who avoid checking statements.
  3. 3.Households of secrecy produce adults who do not discuss money with partners.
  4. 4.Households of shame produce adults who under-earn to stay invisible.
  5. 5.Households of control produce adults who confuse spending with freedom.
  6. 6.Households of neglect produce adults who treat money as fictional.
  7. 7.Households of performance produce adults who buy access they did not need.
  8. 8.Households of austerity produce adults who cannot enjoy what they have.
  9. 9.Households of crisis produce adults who treat normal months as suspicious.
  10. 10.Households of plenty produce adults who underestimate the cost of ordinary risk.
The childhood economy is the operating system the adult one runs on.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-19
Bone

Eight Signs You Confuse Austerity With Virtue

  1. 1.You feel morally superior after declining a normal purchase.
  2. 2.You resent people who enjoy what they have earned.
  3. 3.You postpone basic comforts long after the budget could absorb them.
  4. 4.You feel guilty during pleasurable experiences as they happen.
  5. 5.You judge others' spending in domains you have decided are excessive.
  6. 6.You frame suffering as character rather than as a state to address.
  7. 7.You boast indirectly about how little you spend on yourself.
  8. 8.Your frugality costs your relationships more than it saves you in dollars.
Discomfort is not the same as discipline.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-20
Sand

Nine Phrases That Reveal Money Trauma

  1. 1.I do not need much — as identity rather than as preference.
  2. 2.Money is not for people like us — class story dressed as personal preference.
  3. 3.Rich people are evil — moralizing a category to avoid examining the wound.
  4. 4.I will figure out a way — chronic avoidance of clean planning.
  5. 5.I am just not that kind of person — about basic financial competence.
  6. 6.Talking about money is tacky — protecting the silence that produced the trauma.
  7. 7.I do not want to be greedy — confusing self-advocacy with greed.
  8. 8.God will provide — outsourcing the agency the situation requires.
  9. 9.Whatever the cost — about decisions that needed a real budget.
Listen to what someone says about money. It is rarely about money.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-21
Ember

Ten Phrases That Reveal Someone Is Lying About Caring

  1. 1.I am here for you — without ever asking what you need.
  2. 2.Let me know if you need anything — placing the burden of asking on you.
  3. 3.I have been thinking about you — without reaching out before they said so.
  4. 4.I would have called but I have been so busy — every time, for years.
  5. 5.I am sorry that happened to you — moving on to their own topic immediately.
  6. 6.I knew you would handle it — as license to not have shown up.
  7. 7.You are the strongest person I know — as license to bring you their problems.
  8. 8.I would never let that happen to you — about hypotheticals that cost them nothing.
  9. 9.I am always rooting for you — said in public, never in private.
  10. 10.I hope you know how much I love you — instead of any concrete demonstration.
Care is a verb. Hear the syntax.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-22
Midnight

Nine Things To Stop Saying During Conflict

  1. 1.You always — globalizing one event into a character verdict.
  2. 2.You never — same move, opposite valence.
  3. 3.Just like last time — recapitulating an older fight inside the current one.
  4. 4.Calm down — instructing the other person to feel less while you feel more.
  5. 5.If you really loved me — converting the disagreement into a loyalty test.
  6. 6.You are being too sensitive — denying the legitimacy of the response.
  7. 7.Whatever — retreating to deny the conflict ever happened.
  8. 8.I am done talking about this — closing the door mid-conversation.
  9. 9.You sound just like your mother / father — invoking ancestry as ammunition.
These phrases extend the conflict by changing what is being argued about.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-23
Tobacco

Eight Ways To Know A Friendship Is Performative

  1. 1.The photographs outnumber the actual conversations.
  2. 2.The public posts about each other are warmer than the private texts.
  3. 3.Crisis moments produce sympathy emojis but no actual presence.
  4. 4.Plans are cancelled casually but birthdays are celebrated theatrically.
  5. 5.They tag you in content about friendship more than they sit with you.
  6. 6.Your name appears in captions but rarely in their actual calendar.
  7. 7.They defend the friendship publicly when nobody had attacked it.
  8. 8.You realize you have no idea what they are actually struggling with.
Some friendships are real. Some are performances of real friendships.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-24
Candle

Nine Signs You Are Being Talked At, Not To

  1. 1.Their pauses feel like loading rather than listening.
  2. 2.They respond to your sentence by continuing their own thought.
  3. 3.They ask no follow-up questions to anything you say.
  4. 4.They redirect to themselves within one minute of any topic you raise.
  5. 5.They interrupt but treat your interruptions as rudeness.
  6. 6.Their stories arrive prepared, with practiced pacing and rehearsed lines.
  7. 7.They check their phone the moment you start speaking.
  8. 8.You finish the conversation exhausted rather than energized.
  9. 9.You realize they could have had the same conversation alone.
Conversation is reciprocal by definition. Monologue dressed as dialogue is data.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-25
Ash

Eight Phrases That Defuse Hostile Rooms

  1. 1.Help me understand what you mean by that — buys time without conceding ground.
  2. 2.That is fair, and here is what I am holding — acknowledgment plus continuation.
  3. 3.Let us slow this down for a minute — pacing as intervention.
  4. 4.I am not going to respond to that right now — declining the bait, not the room.
  5. 5.Can we agree on the facts first? — separating data from interpretation.
  6. 6.I hear you. I do not agree — closing the listening loop without folding.
  7. 7.Let us come back to this when we have both eaten — naming physiology as a variable.
  8. 8.That is a serious accusation. Walk me through it slowly — making rhetoric pay its tax.
De-escalation is a vocabulary. These are the load-bearing words.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-26
Dusk

Eight Signs Your Nervous System Never Came Down

  1. 1.You wake tired regardless of how many hours you slept.
  2. 2.Small noises make you flinch in a way they did not five years ago.
  3. 3.You feel a low hum of dread you cannot trace to any one source.
  4. 4.Your jaw is clenched by mid-morning without your noticing.
  5. 5.You sleep best when exhausted to the point of collapse.
  6. 6.Your breath lives in your shoulders, not your belly.
  7. 7.You feel suspicious of calm moments rather than restored by them.
  8. 8.Rest produces guilt rather than relief.
The body keeps watching long after the threat is gone.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-27
Iron

Nine Things That Look Like Depression But Are Not

  1. 1.Burnout from chronic over-functioning, not depression.
  2. 2.Grief that has not been given language or time.
  3. 3.Anemia or thyroid dysfunction producing affective flattening.
  4. 4.Sleep apnea draining cognition while you blame your mood.
  5. 5.Vitamin D deficiency in low-light seasons or indoor lives.
  6. 6.Cumulative isolation dressed as introversion.
  7. 7.Disordered eating producing mood symptoms downstream.
  8. 8.Misaligned career draining vitality you blame on yourself.
  9. 9.Bereavement delayed by months of necessary functioning.
Different roots require different responses. Misdiagnosis costs years.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-28
Velvet

Ten Signs You Are Dissociating In Real Time

  1. 1.You feel like you are watching the conversation from across the room.
  2. 2.Sounds become muffled during emotionally loaded moments.
  3. 3.Time seems to skip — twenty minutes pass like five.
  4. 4.You lose access to vocabulary you normally have.
  5. 5.Your hands feel like they belong to someone else.
  6. 6.You agree to things without registering the content of what was asked.
  7. 7.You feel a strange calm during conversations that should activate you.
  8. 8.Your body feels distant — pain, hunger, fatigue dim out.
  9. 9.You cannot recall how you got from one room to another.
  10. 10.You return to yourself hours later with no clear memory of what happened.
Dissociation is not weakness. It is a learned defense, still running.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-29
Bone

Eight Ways Your Body Reports Trauma You Filed Away

  1. 1.Unexplained tightness in the chest at specific times of year.
  2. 2.Recurring dreams that feature the same physical sensation.
  3. 3.Sudden nausea triggered by a song, a scent, a phrase.
  4. 4.Persistent pain in regions doctors have cleared structurally.
  5. 5.Startle responses disproportionate to the sound.
  6. 6.Headaches clustered around anniversaries you have not consciously tracked.
  7. 7.Skin conditions flaring during specific kinds of conflict.
  8. 8.Gut problems that map to certain people more than certain foods.
The mind forgets. The body remembers, on schedule.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-30
Sand

Nine Patterns Of High-Functioning Depression

  1. 1.You meet every deadline and feel nothing upon completion.
  2. 2.You receive praise that lands flat against an interior you cannot name.
  3. 3.You socialize exactly as much as social expectations require, no more.
  4. 4.You go through daily routines with mechanical precision and no presence.
  5. 5.You feel most alive during emergencies because emergencies require less acting.
  6. 6.Your interior monologue is steady, low-grade, and unending.
  7. 7.You answer I'm fine faster than any honest reflection would allow.
  8. 8.You wonder, privately, whether anyone would notice if you stopped trying.
  9. 9.You have stopped anticipating anything specific in the future.
Functioning is not evidence of wellness. It is sometimes the disguise.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-31
Ember

Eight Signs You Raised Yourself

  1. 1.You taught yourself the adult skills nobody walked you through.
  2. 2.You read social rules by observation because no one explained them.
  3. 3.You learned to cook, schedule, manage money without instruction.
  4. 4.You parented your own emotional reactions because no one held them.
  5. 5.You celebrated your own milestones because no one else did.
  6. 6.You feel competent but rarely supported.
  7. 7.You feel uncomfortable receiving help even when you asked for it.
  8. 8.You assume the adult in the room is you, even when you are the youngest.
The competence is real. The childhood was also real.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-32
Midnight

Nine Ways You Mother Yourself Now That No One Did Then

  1. 1.You make yourself eat when the day has been too much to do it.
  2. 2.You put yourself to bed at a reasonable hour because no one else will.
  3. 3.You celebrate your own small wins with deliberate ritual.
  4. 4.You forgive yourself in language no one taught you.
  5. 5.You check on your tone with yourself in the way you wish a parent had.
  6. 6.You speak to your tired body the way you would speak to a child.
  7. 7.You plan rest before exhaustion arrives, the way good parents plan naps.
  8. 8.You protect your own time from people who would not have asked twice.
  9. 9.You witness yourself doing hard things, because no one else is there to.
The repair is uneven, daily, and undertaken alone.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-33
Tobacco

Eight Signs You Have A Parentified Inner Child

  1. 1.You feel responsible for other adults' emotional regulation reflexively.
  2. 2.You anticipate needs no one asked you to anticipate.
  3. 3.You feel guilty declining requests that should not have been made.
  4. 4.You are exhausted by social events that other people find restorative.
  5. 5.You feel uncomfortable being cared for unless you have earned it.
  6. 6.You give advice before being asked because giving advice was your job at age nine.
  7. 7.You feel most yourself when others are leaning on you, and lost when they are not.
  8. 8.Your friends describe you as wise beyond your years — and you suspect the cost.
The child took the role early. The child is still on duty.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-34
Candle

Nine Patterns Adult Children Of Alcoholics Share

  1. 1.You can read a room's mood within seconds of entering it.
  2. 2.You assume responsibility for managing others' emotional weather.
  3. 3.You feel uneasy during periods of stable calm.
  4. 4.You overreact to unpredictability in ways that confuse current loved ones.
  5. 5.You confuse intensity with intimacy.
  6. 6.You apologize first in conflicts you did not start.
  7. 7.You feel guilty for happiness you cannot trace to anyone giving you permission.
  8. 8.You judge yourself by whether you are too much before others have weighed in.
  9. 9.You attract partners who recreate the dynamic, then wonder why.
The disease was theirs. The adaptations became yours.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-35
Ash

Eight Things You Still Do At Thirty-Five That You Did At Five

  1. 1.You brace before opening certain kinds of communication.
  2. 2.You flinch slightly when someone reaches toward your face.
  3. 3.You apologize before stating a need you have every right to state.
  4. 4.You track the moods of the people closest to you with subconscious precision.
  5. 5.You suppress tears in situations where tears are appropriate.
  6. 6.You finish what is on your plate even after you are full.
  7. 7.You feel guilty taking the last of something even when offered.
  8. 8.You hide the parts of yourself that the original audience disapproved of.
Some reflexes were trained early and never updated.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-36
Dusk

Eight Red Flags In Month One Of Dating

  1. 1.They speed the relationship markers — exclusivity, meet the family, move in.
  2. 2.They tell you everyone in their past was crazy or terrible.
  3. 3.They love-bomb with rare amounts of praise in the first two weeks.
  4. 4.They get visibly upset when you spend a night not responding.
  5. 5.They become cold the first time you cannot accommodate them.
  6. 6.They are intensely affectionate in private and slightly distant in public.
  7. 7.They correct you in front of others, then call it teasing.
  8. 8.They have no friends from any previous chapter of their life.
The audition is also a tell. Watch what they show you.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-37
Iron

Nine Red Flags In Month Three Of Dating

  1. 1.The frequency of contact drops without acknowledgment.
  2. 2.Plans you set get rescheduled casually after being firm before.
  3. 3.Their patience with small things narrows sharply.
  4. 4.Their family turns out to be more central than they implied.
  5. 5.Their ex keeps appearing in casual stories.
  6. 6.Disagreements escalate faster than they did in month one.
  7. 7.You catch yourself editing what you say to avoid certain reactions.
  8. 8.Their jokes about you start to land with a small bite.
  9. 9.Their generosity becomes conditional in ways it was not.
Month three is when the rehearsed person tires of rehearsing.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-38
Velvet

Eight Signs You Are Settling Without Knowing It

  1. 1.You describe the relationship by what it is not, more than what it is.
  2. 2.Your friends have stopped asking enthusiastic follow-ups.
  3. 3.You defend the relationship more than you enjoy it.
  4. 4.You plan milestones based on age, not on connection.
  5. 5.You grade the relationship on a curve based on past worse ones.
  6. 6.You feel relieved, not delighted, when they show up.
  7. 7.You catch yourself bargaining about which version of love is realistic.
  8. 8.You imagine your future and the partner is generic, not specific.
Settling rarely arrives as a choice. It arrives as a fade.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-39
Bone

Nine Signs The Attraction Is Anxiety, Not Love

  1. 1.You feel most attracted when you do not know where you stand.
  2. 2.Calm responses from them feel like rejection.
  3. 3.You are most certain about the relationship during fights.
  4. 4.Your interest spikes when they pull back and flattens when they pursue.
  5. 5.You cannot relax in their presence even during good moments.
  6. 6.You feel euphoric after reconciliation, regardless of what was reconciled.
  7. 7.You anticipate every meeting with a physiological surge that does not fade.
  8. 8.You think about them most when the situation is uncertain.
  9. 9.Stable affection bores you within weeks.
The nervous system can mistake threat for chemistry.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-40
Sand

Eight Ways Healthy Partners Argue

  1. 1.They name the topic clearly and stay on it.
  2. 2.They use I-statements without rehearsing them.
  3. 3.They acknowledge what is true in the other's complaint before continuing.
  4. 4.They pause when escalation begins, by mutual agreement.
  5. 5.They are willing to be wrong in real time.
  6. 6.They do not invoke old grievances as ammunition.
  7. 7.They repair during, not after — small acknowledgments mid-argument.
  8. 8.They end the disagreement with a plan, not a verdict on each other.
The conflict has the same content. The form is entirely different.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-41
Ember

Eight Cognitive Biases That Ruin Decisions

  1. 1.Sunk cost — continuing because of what was already spent.
  2. 2.Confirmation bias — only noticing evidence for what you already believe.
  3. 3.Recency bias — overweighting the last data point over the long pattern.
  4. 4.Availability bias — confusing easy-to-remember with frequent.
  5. 5.Anchoring — being unduly influenced by the first number or framing.
  6. 6.Loss aversion — fearing loss more than equivalent gain motivates you.
  7. 7.Optimism bias — assuming the schedule will hold for you uniquely.
  8. 8.Halo effect — letting one strong impression color unrelated judgments.
The brain is reliable in the ways it is reliably wrong.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-42
Midnight

Nine Signs You Are Rationalizing What You Already Decided

  1. 1.You catch yourself building a case to one specific person in your head.
  2. 2.Every counter-argument finds a new defeater within minutes.
  3. 3.You stop consulting the friend whose pushback would matter most.
  4. 4.Your list of reasons keeps growing without the conclusion changing.
  5. 5.You time-pressure the decision to prevent further input.
  6. 6.You frame the decision as already in motion before it is.
  7. 7.You avoid writing the decision down in plain language.
  8. 8.Your body tightens during the rehearsal of the reasons.
  9. 9.You feel defensive discussing the decision before anyone has attacked it.
The decision was made. The arguments are the evidence locker after the fact.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-43
Tobacco

Eight Patterns Of Analysis Paralysis

  1. 1.You research the same question across forty browser tabs.
  2. 2.You add new variables to the decision every week, indefinitely.
  3. 3.You consult one more person before each near-decision.
  4. 4.You defer the choice because you have not yet read the definitive book.
  5. 5.You frame indecision as due diligence when the diligence is done.
  6. 6.You postpone small choices that should take seconds.
  7. 7.You feel physically tired after research sessions with no output.
  8. 8.You convince yourself the right time is just past the next variable.
Endless deliberation is not careful thinking. It is avoidance dressed as rigor.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-44
Candle

Nine Ways To Test A Feeling Before Acting On It

  1. 1.Wait forty-eight hours and see if the feeling remains the same shape.
  2. 2.Write the feeling in one sentence on paper.
  3. 3.Move the body — walk, run, swim — and check the feeling afterward.
  4. 4.Eat and sleep, then check whether the urgency persisted.
  5. 5.Describe the feeling to one trusted person and watch their face.
  6. 6.Imagine the action playing out and notice the secondary feeling that arrives.
  7. 7.Reverse the framing — what would I do if I felt the opposite?
  8. 8.Ask the part of you that argues against this — what is it protecting?
  9. 9.Wait until the feeling is boring. Decide from there.
Feelings are data. They are not directives.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-45
Ash

Eight Signs Your Decision Is Actually A Reaction

  1. 1.It arrived within minutes of an emotional event.
  2. 2.It involves cutting off someone permanently after one incident.
  3. 3.It is bigger than the situation that triggered it.
  4. 4.You feel a physiological surge while making it.
  5. 5.You cannot state the reasons in calm prose.
  6. 6.You are choosing it to win against someone specific, not to gain something.
  7. 7.You feel urgency to execute it before you change your mind.
  8. 8.Friends who know you well are alarmed by the speed.
Reaction wears the clothing of decision. Watch the timing.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-46
Dusk

Eight Things Narcissists Do During Their Hoover Phase

  1. 1.They arrive with a dramatic apology that does not name the specific harms.
  2. 2.They invoke shared history you cannot defend against without seeming cold.
  3. 3.They reach out through mutual friends who carry the message on their behalf.
  4. 4.They time the return for a moment they know you are vulnerable.
  5. 5.They reference a crisis of their own that requires your attention.
  6. 6.They offer gifts, gestures, or words engineered to bypass your defenses.
  7. 7.They claim transformation that has not yet shown up in any concrete behavior.
  8. 8.They become cold or hostile the moment you decline to engage.
The return is engineered. Watch the structure.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-47
Iron

Nine Patterns Of Weaponized Incompetence

  1. 1.They cannot find anything in shared spaces despite living there for years.
  2. 2.They do the task so poorly that you redo it and they avoid the next round.
  3. 3.They claim to be bad at the specific things that fall to them by default.
  4. 4.They forget the steps each time, requiring re-training that exhausts you.
  5. 5.They take three times as long as anyone else, ensuring you intervene.
  6. 6.They produce crises that require your competence to resolve.
  7. 7.Their claimed incompetence disappears when it benefits them.
  8. 8.They praise your skill at the thing as cover for not learning it.
  9. 9.They frame the imbalance as personality, not avoidance.
Inability dressed as a personality trait is a strategy.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-48
Velvet

Eight Signs You Are Being Gray-Rocked By Family

  1. 1.Short answers arrive where conversation used to.
  2. 2.Plans are made around you, not with you.
  3. 3.You hear significant news through other family members.
  4. 4.Topics that used to invite engagement now get one-word responses.
  5. 5.They are polite in a way they never were when you were close.
  6. 6.You catch yourself fishing for any reaction at all.
  7. 7.Holidays are coordinated in your absence and your presence is logistical.
  8. 8.There is no fight to recover from. Just a closed door dressed as politeness.
When someone makes you boring on purpose, you have lost access.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-49
Bone

Nine Ways Manipulators Co-Opt Your Support System

  1. 1.They become charming to your closest friend or sibling.
  2. 2.They confide in your support people, casting themselves as the wronged party.
  3. 3.They recruit mutual friends by sharing their version first.
  4. 4.They send your family gifts and warm messages independent of you.
  5. 5.They make your friends feel useful in the relationship's repair.
  6. 6.They tell your people details about your behavior, framed as concern.
  7. 7.They show up at events your support network attends, without you.
  8. 8.They cultivate one-on-one relationships your friends do not tell you about.
  9. 9.They use your therapist references against you in arguments.
The flying monkey was a friend before they were a tool.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-50
Sand

Eight Signs You Are Dating A Chronic Victim

  1. 1.Every previous relationship ended because the other person was wrong.
  2. 2.Every workplace had a boss who failed to see their value.
  3. 3.Every friendship that ended was someone else's betrayal.
  4. 4.Their family is uniquely unfair in a way that defies probability.
  5. 5.They have a story about being misunderstood for every situation.
  6. 6.Sympathy from you is consumed but rarely reciprocated.
  7. 7.Concrete fixes for their problems are rejected with new objections.
  8. 8.Their crises cluster whenever attention drifts elsewhere.
Some pain is real. Some pain is the role.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-51
Ember

Eight Phrases That Signal A Toxic Startup Culture

  1. 1.We work hard, we play hard — code for boundary erosion in both directions.
  2. 2.We are a family here — code for unpaid emotional labor and confused authority.
  3. 3.Fast-paced environment — code for chronic understaffing.
  4. 4.Wear many hats — code for three jobs at one salary.
  5. 5.Move fast and break things — code for no quality controls and no accountability.
  6. 6.We do not believe in titles — code for promotions that never arrive.
  7. 7.Our culture is unique — code for behaviors that would not survive scrutiny elsewhere.
  8. 8.Founder energy — code for accepting unreasonable behavior from leadership.
The pitch is the warning. Listen to what they brag about.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-52
Midnight

Nine Patterns Of Meeting Hijacking

  1. 1.They redirect to their pet topic within five minutes.
  2. 2.They invoke a hypothetical to derail a concrete decision.
  3. 3.They call out a single tactical point to avoid the strategic question.
  4. 4.They speak the longest about the items they understand the least.
  5. 5.They interrupt a more junior speaker to add nothing.
  6. 6.They force a sidebar that becomes the new agenda.
  7. 7.They appeal to a senior person not present to overturn the room.
  8. 8.They postpone the actual decision by demanding more data.
  9. 9.They summarize the meeting in a way that favors their position over what was agreed.
The agenda was hijacked before the meeting started.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-53
Tobacco

Eight Signs You Are Being Managed Out

  1. 1.Stretch assignments stop arriving.
  2. 2.Visibility to senior leadership quietly decreases.
  3. 3.Your one-on-ones get rescheduled or shortened.
  4. 4.Feedback shifts from coaching tone to documentation tone.
  5. 5.Peers stop being staffed on your projects.
  6. 6.Cross-functional meetings start happening without your invitation.
  7. 7.Your manager stops advocating for you in calibration discussions.
  8. 8.Your PIP arrives framed as developmental, not punitive.
The exit was decided before you were told. Recognize the choreography.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-54
Candle

Nine Things Financially Literate Professionals Do Quietly

  1. 1.They automate savings before they see the paycheck.
  2. 2.They invest steadily through downturns instead of timing markets.
  3. 3.They negotiate every offer, including ones from people they like.
  4. 4.They keep low-key lifestyles relative to their income.
  5. 5.They track net worth quarterly, not monthly.
  6. 6.They separate tax accounts the day income arrives.
  7. 7.They refuse loans to family members, knowing the relationship survives better.
  8. 8.They postpone big purchases until past the desire phase.
  9. 9.They talk about money concretely with their partner without drama.
Money mastery is invisible. The performances are usually amateurs.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-55
Ash

Eight Signs You Outgrew Your Job Eighteen Months Before Leaving

  1. 1.You stopped learning anything new and started repeating skills you had.
  2. 2.Your enthusiasm for new projects drained without an obvious cause.
  3. 3.You felt cynical about leadership decisions you used to defend.
  4. 4.Sunday started feeling heavier each week.
  5. 5.Conversations with peers shifted from strategy to politics.
  6. 6.You started reading job listings on idle lunch breaks.
  7. 7.Your body felt better on weekends in a way it had not before.
  8. 8.Your interior stopped writing the next chapter at this company.
The data showed up early. The decision arrived late.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-56
Dusk

Nine Things That Make People Trust You Faster

  1. 1.You remember small details from the previous conversation.
  2. 2.You admit what you do not know without inflating it as humility.
  3. 3.You defend people who are not in the room.
  4. 4.You protect private information when asked, without negotiation.
  5. 5.You change your opinion when given better evidence, visibly.
  6. 6.You follow up on small commitments before being reminded.
  7. 7.You credit others for ideas in front of senior people.
  8. 8.You deliver hard messages without softening past the point of clarity.
  9. 9.You do not triangulate — what you say about A in front of B is consistent.
Trust accelerates around specific behaviors. None of them are flattery.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-57
Iron

Eight Signs You Have Stopped Really Listening

  1. 1.You catch yourself rehearsing what to say while they are still speaking.
  2. 2.You check your phone during pauses in their story.
  3. 3.You interrupt to redirect rather than to clarify.
  4. 4.You ask no follow-up questions because you have stopped being curious.
  5. 5.You summarize their position back inaccurately, in a way that favors yours.
  6. 6.You realize you cannot recall their answer from earlier in the same conversation.
  7. 7.You anticipate the end of their sentences and stop tracking the actual words.
  8. 8.You feel bored by their stories before they have started telling them.
Listening decayed in increments. The decay is reversible if named.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-58
Velvet

Nine Ways To Spot A One-Upper In Conversation

  1. 1.They match your story with a bigger version of the same story.
  2. 2.They redirect to themselves within thirty seconds of any topic.
  3. 3.Their version of your experience is always more intense or more impressive.
  4. 4.They minimize your achievement before sharing a comparable one of theirs.
  5. 5.They casually mention credentials that were not relevant.
  6. 6.Their struggles are framed as more severe than yours.
  7. 7.Their recovery from any difficulty is always quicker than yours.
  8. 8.Their advice to you is built around their own past success.
  9. 9.You leave conversations feeling smaller without being able to point at why.
Every story is a competition for them. The competition runs in silence.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-59
Bone

Eight Things Charismatic People Do That Look Effortless

  1. 1.They do not fill silences that the other person could fill.
  2. 2.They do not explain a reference before being asked to.
  3. 3.They do not laugh at their own jokes for the audience.
  4. 4.They do not perform humility they do not feel.
  5. 5.They do not close every story with the moral.
  6. 6.They do not check their phone in mid-sentence.
  7. 7.They do not qualify every opinion with disclaimers.
  8. 8.They do not compete with the person they are talking to.
Charisma is mostly subtraction. These are the things they have stopped doing.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-60
Sand

Nine Phrases That Reveal You Have Been Dismissed

  1. 1.I think you are reading too much into this — denying your perception.
  2. 2.Let us not make this a thing — closing the conversation by labeling it.
  3. 3.That is interesting — said in the tone reserved for irrelevant.
  4. 4.Mmhm — paired with no eye contact.
  5. 5.We will see — applied to ideas you cared about.
  6. 6.Anyway, moving on — used right after your contribution.
  7. 7.Sure, whatever you think — capitulation that ends the dialogue.
  8. 8.That is one way of looking at it — implying the wrong one.
  9. 9.Got it — said before you have actually been understood.
The dismissal arrived in language designed to deny it happened.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-61
Ember

Eight Signs Your Body Is Running On Cortisol

  1. 1.You crash during the first hour after the workday ends.
  2. 2.You cannot nap, even when exhausted.
  3. 3.Your appetite is irregular — ravenous, then absent, with no pattern.
  4. 4.You feel tired but wired at the end of the day.
  5. 5.Your resting heart rate is higher than it was a year ago.
  6. 6.Caffeine barely works any more.
  7. 7.Alcohol immediately tanks your sleep.
  8. 8.Your immune system picks up every passing virus.
Cortisol works. Until it does not.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-62
Midnight

Nine Things To Stop Doing Before Bed

  1. 1.Bright overhead lighting in the bathroom and bedroom.
  2. 2.Scrolling feeds that engineer outrage and curiosity.
  3. 3.Late caffeine, even afternoon caffeine for slow metabolizers.
  4. 4.Heavy meals within two hours of horizontal.
  5. 5.Difficult conversations with the people you live with.
  6. 6.Mentally rehearsing the next day's hard meeting.
  7. 7.Alcohol — sleeps you fast, wakes you at 3 a.m.
  8. 8.High-intensity exercise within ninety minutes of bed.
  9. 9.Charging your phone within arm's reach.
The first hour of sleep is set by the last hour of waking.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-63
Tobacco

Eight Signs You Are Eating Your Emotions

  1. 1.You eat most reliably an hour after a difficult interaction.
  2. 2.You plan the next meal during the current one.
  3. 3.You feel guilty during meals, even when the food is reasonable.
  4. 4.Your eating schedule has nothing to do with hunger.
  5. 5.Certain foods only appear during specific moods.
  6. 6.You eat faster when alone than when watched.
  7. 7.You feel numb immediately after meals, not satisfied.
  8. 8.You cannot remember what you ate two hours ago.
Hunger is not always hunger. The body uses what is available.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-64
Candle

Nine Patterns Of High-Functioning Burnout

  1. 1.You meet every deadline and feel nothing afterward.
  2. 2.You feel resentful of work you used to enjoy.
  3. 3.Small requests feel disproportionately heavy.
  4. 4.You fantasize about minor injuries that would justify rest.
  5. 5.You feel a flat affect during recognition.
  6. 6.Weekend recovery no longer makes Monday feel different.
  7. 7.You postpone enjoyable things you used to fit in.
  8. 8.Cynicism arrives where curiosity used to.
  9. 9.You wonder, in the quiet hour, whether you would notice if you stopped trying.
You are still producing. That is part of the diagnosis, not against it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-65
Ash

Eight Things To Do Daily That Compound Over Decades

  1. 1.Walk outside, briefly, before the day begins.
  2. 2.Drink water before any other beverage.
  3. 3.Move for thirty minutes — any movement, every day.
  4. 4.Read one page of a real book that is not work-related.
  5. 5.Call or message one person you care about, even briefly.
  6. 6.Cook one component of one meal instead of buying all of it.
  7. 7.Write three sentences about what happened today, even badly.
  8. 8.Refuse the phone for the first ten minutes after waking.
Daily is a number that compounds. Annual is a number that decays.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-66
Ember

Eight Things To Ask Before You Commit

  1. 1.How do you fight when tired and when rested? The difference is the truth.
  2. 2.What does your apology look like when you are wrong about something small?
  3. 3.When you have a hard week, do you withdraw or do you reach?
  4. 4.What is your relationship with your own mother and father as it stands now?
  5. 5.How do you handle money disagreements with your closest people?
  6. 6.What do you do when a friend fails you in a meaningful way?
  7. 7.What is your most recent unresolved conflict, and where does it sit now?
  8. 8.When was the last time you changed your mind about something important?
The information is available. Most people decline to gather it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-67
Midnight

Nine Signs The Chemistry Is One-Sided

  1. 1.You initiate eighty percent of contact and plans.
  2. 2.They match your energy briefly, then drop back to baseline.
  3. 3.Their follow-through on your hard moments is consistently low.
  4. 4.They forget details you remember about them within minutes.
  5. 5.Their enthusiasm for the relationship spikes only when threatened.
  6. 6.You feel grateful for behaviors that should be baseline.
  7. 7.Conversations about the relationship end with you apologizing for raising them.
  8. 8.You catch yourself campaigning for the relationship to them.
  9. 9.You realize you are more invested in the future they imagine than they are.
You can be the entire engine of a relationship for years. The realization is sudden.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-68
Tobacco

Eight Phrases That Mean The Relationship Has Already Ended

  1. 1.I just do not have the energy right now — chronic version, not acute.
  2. 2.Let us not make this a big deal — repeated about anything that matters.
  3. 3.You knew what you signed up for — said about reasonable updates.
  4. 4.I am too tired to talk about this again — when the topic has not been resolved.
  5. 5.Maybe we should take some space — said by the one who wants out.
  6. 6.I do not know what you want from me anymore — refusing to ask.
  7. 7.It is what it is — about things that could change with effort.
  8. 8.Whatever — used as the end of a sentence, not the start.
The phrase arrives months before the conversation that ends it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-69
Candle

Nine Things Long-Term Couples Stop Saying

  1. 1.Sorry to bother you — they cannot bother each other.
  2. 2.I do not want to be a burden — the burden frame is gone.
  3. 3.I would never ask you to do that — they ask now.
  4. 4.Are you sure? — the trust is no longer renewed each time.
  5. 5.Please — for small things both have done a thousand times.
  6. 6.I am fine — when not fine, because the lie is exhausting and useless.
  7. 7.That is your problem — even when literally true.
  8. 8.I told you so — it stopped earning anything years ago.
  9. 9.Do you still love me? — the question has answered itself.
Some omissions are erosion. Some omissions are arrival.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-70
Ash

Eight Signs You Are Auditioning Instead Of Dating

  1. 1.You manage your reactions in advance to avoid scaring them.
  2. 2.You hide opinions they might not share.
  3. 3.You edit your week to make it sound more interesting.
  4. 4.You adopt their interests as your own, fast.
  5. 5.You minimize the people in your life who would make them uneasy.
  6. 6.You feel anxious during silences because you might be losing the role.
  7. 7.You cancel plans to be available when they suggest something.
  8. 8.You spend the day after a date analyzing your performance.
The audition cannot become a relationship. The audition only ends.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-71
Dusk

Eight Lies You Tell Yourself About Why You Stayed

  1. 1.It is not that bad — the comparison set is silently rigged.
  2. 2.I am the only one who can handle them — the savior frame as cover.
  3. 3.They are going through something — for the seventh year.
  4. 4.Leaving would be unfair to them right now — the right moment never arrives.
  5. 5.Nobody else would put up with this from me — projected self-judgment.
  6. 6.I do not want to be the kind of person who quits — virtue as captivity.
  7. 7.The good moments are really good — measured against the worst, not the average.
  8. 8.I have invested too much to leave now — sunk cost dressed as integrity.
The reasons sound coherent. They are reasons after the fact.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-72
Iron

Nine Things You Have Been Calling Patience

  1. 1.Tolerance of behavior you would not tolerate from a stranger.
  2. 2.Avoidance of a conversation you know is required.
  3. 3.Fatigue mislabeled as wisdom.
  4. 4.Hope applied to evidence that does not support it.
  5. 5.Loyalty to a version of someone that no longer exists.
  6. 6.Fear of the discomfort of confrontation.
  7. 7.Inertia dressed in the vocabulary of grace.
  8. 8.Denial of the trajectory you can already see.
  9. 9.Repeating a sentence in your head while waiting for someone to change.
Patience without movement is something else with a kinder name.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-73
Velvet

Eight Signs You Are Performing Your Personality

  1. 1.You rehearse casual stories before social events.
  2. 2.You feel physically tired after spending time with close friends.
  3. 3.You post opinions you privately question.
  4. 4.You laugh at jokes that did not land for you.
  5. 5.You deliver the same anecdote with the same intonation.
  6. 6.Your aesthetic feels assembled rather than arrived at.
  7. 7.You feel most relaxed alone, and that surprises you.
  8. 8.You catch yourself narrating the moment as if for an audience.
Performance leaves residue. The residue is exhaustion.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-74
Bone

Nine Ways You Romanticize Suffering To Avoid Changing It

  1. 1.You frame your struggles as proof of depth that others lack.
  2. 2.You post about hard times in ways that invite admiration.
  3. 3.You resist solutions that would resolve the suffering quickly.
  4. 4.You return to relationships that hurt you in ways you call passionate.
  5. 5.You measure meaning by intensity of feeling, not by outcome.
  6. 6.You suspect people who seem stable of being unexamined.
  7. 7.You narrate your life as a series of redemptive arcs.
  8. 8.You defend your dysfunctions as the source of your sensitivity.
  9. 9.You feel suspicious of contentment, as if it were a betrayal.
Pain as identity is comfortable. Identity is sticky.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-75
Sand

Eight Phrases That Reveal You Are Hiding From Yourself

  1. 1.It is what it is — about situations you have decided not to face.
  2. 2.I do not have time for that right now — said for years about the same thing.
  3. 3.I am just not that kind of person — about behaviors you have not tried.
  4. 4.Other people have it worse — used to invalidate your own data.
  5. 5.Maybe in a few years — about decisions that compound monthly.
  6. 6.I am happy with where I am — said in the same flat tone every time.
  7. 7.That is not really my thing — about things you have never sampled.
  8. 8.I will figure it out eventually — for the thing that has needed figuring for five years.
Listen to your own sentences when no one else is around.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-76
Ember

Eight Things To Stop Explaining To People Who Have Already Decided

  1. 1.Your career choices, to family members invested in a different one.
  2. 2.Your relationship, to people who decided they disapproved years ago.
  3. 3.Your therapy, to anyone who treats it as evidence of weakness.
  4. 4.Your spending on yourself, to people who have not earned that visibility.
  5. 5.Your political views, to people who confuse disagreement with attack.
  6. 6.Your religious practice or its absence.
  7. 7.Your body — its size, its scars, its appetites.
  8. 8.Your decision to limit contact, to the person whose access you are limiting.
Explanation only works on people open to being persuaded. The rest is theater.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-77
Midnight

Nine Phrases That Hold A No Without Apologizing

  1. 1.That does not work for me.
  2. 2.I am not available for that.
  3. 3.I will not be doing that.
  4. 4.That is a no from me.
  5. 5.I am going to pass.
  6. 6.I have already decided.
  7. 7.I do not owe a reason.
  8. 8.The answer will not change.
  9. 9.That is not on the table.
The no needs no scaffolding. The scaffolding invites negotiation.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-78
Tobacco

Eight Signs You Are Being Tested For New Boundaries

  1. 1.They forget the boundary within days, and pretend surprise when reminded.
  2. 2.They negotiate the boundary as if it were a draft for discussion.
  3. 3.They invoke the past, when the boundary was not yet stated, to invalidate it.
  4. 4.They escalate through a flying monkey rather than re-engage directly.
  5. 5.They miniaturize the situation — I just asked one small thing.
  6. 6.They invoke hypotheticals to find the edge of the new rule.
  7. 7.They become cold in response to the boundary, then warm to test if you fold.
  8. 8.They frame their inability to accept the boundary as a personality trait.
The test is rarely announced. Your job is to recognize the test.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-79
Candle

Nine Patterns Of Boundary Erosion In Long Relationships

  1. 1.The exception that becomes the new rule within three months.
  2. 2.The plea that bypasses the limit just this once — for the fourteenth time.
  3. 3.The humor that softens the violation while normalizing it.
  4. 4.The fatigue that makes re-stating the limit feel disproportionate.
  5. 5.The partner's feelings becoming the metric instead of the agreed structure.
  6. 6.The new context that retroactively reopens what was settled.
  7. 7.The bundling of the boundary with other issues, so addressing it requires a fight.
  8. 8.The guilt deployed about how much they need you to bend.
  9. 9.The silence that signals withdrawal until the boundary loosens.
Boundaries do not vanish at once. They are renegotiated by exception.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-80
Ash

Eight Things To Stop Asking Permission For

  1. 1.Spending your own money on things that meet your standards.
  2. 2.Saying no to plans you do not want to attend.
  3. 3.Limiting contact with people who exhaust you.
  4. 4.Changing your career direction at any age.
  5. 5.Ending a friendship that has run its course.
  6. 6.Going to therapy, alone or as a couple.
  7. 7.Disagreeing with your parents on adult decisions.
  8. 8.Resting on a day that is not the weekend.
You are an adult. The license expired with childhood.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-81
Dusk

Eight Signs You Have Been Spiritually Bypassed

  1. 1.You were told to forgive before you had finished registering the harm.
  2. 2.You were told your anger was a low-vibration emotion you needed to release.
  3. 3.Your boundaries were framed as evidence of insufficient love.
  4. 4.Your grief was redirected into talk about purpose and lessons.
  5. 5.Your trauma was reframed as a gift you were chosen to receive.
  6. 6.Your therapy was characterized as a less evolved approach.
  7. 7.Your normal reactions were called ego, while bypassing was called growth.
  8. 8.You were told to meditate on situations that required leaving them.
Spirituality used as anesthesia is the most expensive painkiller available.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-82
Iron

Nine Phrases That Wear Healing Language To Avoid The Work

  1. 1.I am holding space for that — for things they do not want to engage.
  2. 2.That is your stuff to work through — refusing accountability dressed as wisdom.
  3. 3.I am protecting my peace — selective non-engagement with hard truths.
  4. 4.Not my circus, not my monkeys — about commitments they actually made.
  5. 5.I have done the work — without specifying what work, with whom, or how recently.
  6. 6.That is below my frequency — disregarding feedback as a vibrational issue.
  7. 7.I have detached with love — from the topic, the conversation, and the accountability.
  8. 8.You are projecting — to deflect a fair observation.
  9. 9.Sending you light — as a way to end a conversation that demands more.
The vocabulary is correct. The behavior is the same as before.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-83
Velvet

Eight Things Therapy-Speak Has Hidden, Not Healed

  1. 1.Naming a trauma without changing the patterns it produced.
  2. 2.Diagnosing others to avoid examining your part.
  3. 3.Performing awareness while continuing the same behavior.
  4. 4.Citing attachment styles to justify a pattern of harm.
  5. 5.Using boundary as a synonym for control of someone else's behavior.
  6. 6.Quoting therapists in arguments to win, not to understand.
  7. 7.Posting healing content while avoiding the actual hard conversations.
  8. 8.Treating fluency in psychology vocabulary as evidence of integration.
The label is not the work. The work is what the label sometimes replaces.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-84
Bone

Nine Ways Positive Thinking Has Become A Defense

  1. 1.You suppress legitimate fears that are pointing at real problems.
  2. 2.You redirect others' difficult emotions into gratitude exercises.
  3. 3.You interpret every setback as a lesson rather than letting some setbacks just be costs.
  4. 4.You feel guilty for negative thoughts, then guilty for the guilt.
  5. 5.You avoid people whose realism feels heavy to you.
  6. 6.You frame every relationship problem as your need to grow.
  7. 7.You refuse to acknowledge anger because it conflicts with the self-image.
  8. 8.You believe the universe is responding to your specific frequency, despite no evidence.
  9. 9.You use affirmations as a substitute for the actions the affirmations point to.
Forced positivity is denial in better packaging.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-85
Sand

Eight Signs Your Healing Journey Has Become A Performance

  1. 1.You post about healing more than you actually do it.
  2. 2.You quote therapeutic insights to people who did not ask.
  3. 3.You frame every interaction through the language of your own progress.
  4. 4.Your content about healing receives more attention than your relationships do.
  5. 5.You narrate breakthroughs to an audience as they happen.
  6. 6.You introduce yourself by the work you are doing, not by your life.
  7. 7.Your therapist is mentioned in conversations to credentialize positions.
  8. 8.You feel deflated on weeks when the healing has no public surface.
Healing is private work. Performance is a different industry.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-86
Ember

Eight Phrases That Reveal Anger You Have Not Faced

  1. 1.They never listened to me anyway — about people no longer present.
  2. 2.It does not even matter now — said with intensity that contradicts the words.
  3. 3.I am just done — about a topic you have refused to actually process.
  4. 4.Whatever, I am over it — followed by long disproportionate stories.
  5. 5.I am not angry, I am disappointed — the disappointment is anger that lost its passport.
  6. 6.That is just how I am wired — explaining reactivity as constitution.
  7. 7.They are not worth my energy — said about people who have all of your energy.
  8. 8.I have moved on — said too many times to be true.
The anger is older than the situation. Name what it is actually about.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-87
Midnight

Nine Signs Your Anger Is Actually Grief

  1. 1.The rage is disproportionate to the current trigger.
  2. 2.You feel hollow immediately after expressing the anger.
  3. 3.The anger keeps returning to the same theme regardless of the surface topic.
  4. 4.You feel physical heaviness, not heat — a different signal than rage.
  5. 5.You catch yourself avoiding photographs, anniversaries, certain rooms.
  6. 6.Sleep is disrupted in ways that started after a specific loss.
  7. 7.You feel guilty the moment the anger passes, with no clear referent.
  8. 8.You cannot finish crying once you start, because the anger keeps interrupting.
  9. 9.The anger softens only when you say the name of what you have lost.
Grief sometimes arrives wearing borrowed clothes. Look at what is underneath.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-88
Tobacco

Eight Things To Stop Doing When You Are Furious

  1. 1.Sending the email that has been drafted in your head all morning.
  2. 2.Texting the person — anything — in the first thirty minutes.
  3. 3.Posting publicly about the situation, however indirectly.
  4. 4.Calling a third party to recruit them to your view.
  5. 5.Making major decisions about housing, work, or money.
  6. 6.Drinking to take the edge off, then making the call.
  7. 7.Replaying the inciting moment, which keeps the system activated.
  8. 8.Promising the closest people something to prove your loyalty in the heat.
The decisions made in fury must be paid for in calm.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-89
Candle

Eight Signs You Are Hiding From Solitude

  1. 1.You play something the moment you enter an empty room.
  2. 2.Your phone is in your hand within seconds of any pause.
  3. 3.You schedule plans through every weekend, indefinitely.
  4. 4.Your meditation is twenty minutes of fidgeting and self-narration.
  5. 5.You resent your partner for needing alone time, framing it as rejection.
  6. 6.Driving without audio feels uncomfortable in a way you cannot articulate.
  7. 7.You feel a faint panic when an evening clears unexpectedly.
  8. 8.You cannot recall the last time you enjoyed doing nothing.
Solitude is information about yourself. Some people avoid the information for decades.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-90
Ash

Nine Things The First Year Alone Teaches You

  1. 1.Your real preferences, separate from the compromises of cohabitation.
  2. 2.Your actual rhythms of sleep, food, social energy.
  3. 3.Which friendships survive when there is no romantic partner brokering the contact.
  4. 4.Which rooms of your interior life you avoided.
  5. 5.Your capacity to hold silence without filling it.
  6. 6.Your competence with logistics you used to outsource.
  7. 7.Your patience with your own company, or its absence.
  8. 8.Your relationship with weekends as units of time.
  9. 9.Which thoughts you have when nobody is offering an alternative.
Solo living is not isolation. It is a curriculum you cannot take while partnered.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-91
Dusk

Eight Patterns Of Dependence You Mistook For Love

  1. 1.You cannot make a routine decision without consulting them.
  2. 2.Your mood tracks theirs with no independent variation.
  3. 3.Your calendar runs on theirs by default, not by negotiation.
  4. 4.You feel anxious during ordinary separations of hours, not days.
  5. 5.Your identity has reorganized around the relationship's stability.
  6. 6.You have withdrawn from interests that used to fund your sense of self.
  7. 7.You feel embarrassed having opinions they would not share.
  8. 8.You cannot picture a future that does not include them as the central organizing fact.
Need and love overlap. They are not the same shape.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-92
Iron

Nine Signs Grief Has Gone Underground

  1. 1.Irritability with no obvious cause that lasts months.
  2. 2.Loss of interest in things you used to love, without depression's heaviness.
  3. 3.Avoiding music, films, or places associated with the person.
  4. 4.Difficulty concentrating on long tasks, with no other change in your life.
  5. 5.Physical exhaustion that sleep does not address.
  6. 6.Hypersensitivity to other people's losses, in disproportion.
  7. 7.Numbness during what should be meaningful moments in your own life.
  8. 8.Sudden crying in unrelated situations, then a return to flatness.
  9. 9.A low dread before sleep that has no specific content.
Buried grief still does work. It just bills the wrong account.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-93
Velvet

Eight Things To Do After A Death That Compound For Years

  1. 1.Save the voicemails before the phone updates and deletes them.
  2. 2.Download the text threads in full, with timestamps.
  3. 3.Photograph their handwriting — notes, lists, signatures.
  4. 4.Write down the stories you can still remember in their voice.
  5. 5.Keep one piece of their clothing unwashed for years longer than seems sane.
  6. 6.Ask their oldest friends for the stories you do not know.
  7. 7.Visit the places they loved, alone, in the first year.
  8. 8.Tell the people who knew them what the loss has been costing you, specifically.
Some decisions cost five minutes. Their absence costs decades.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-94
Bone

Nine Phrases That Reveal Unfinished Grief

  1. 1.They would have liked this — said with a flatness that is not peace.
  2. 2.It has been a while — about a loss that still organizes your year.
  3. 3.I am over it — repeated as if to convince a panel.
  4. 4.I do not want to make it about me — at funerals and anniversaries where it is appropriate.
  5. 5.It is fine — said in a tone that is not fine.
  6. 6.I do not want to talk about that anymore — about the most important loss of your life.
  7. 7.I should be past this by now — applying a schedule to grief that does not honor one.
  8. 8.They would not want me to be sad — outsourcing your grief to the deceased.
  9. 9.I just keep going — for years, on autopilot.
The grief is still talking. You have stopped listening.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-95
Sand

Eight Things To Stop Optimizing In Your Life

  1. 1.Your friendships — they cannot be batch-processed.
  2. 2.Your grief — the schedule belongs to the loss, not you.
  3. 3.Your creative work — efficiency is not the same as quality.
  4. 4.Your mornings — not every hour needs to compound something.
  5. 5.Your meals — eating as logistics produces a hollow body.
  6. 6.Your conversations — the best ones are off-schedule and unmonetized.
  7. 7.Your attention to the people you love — they are not a productivity input.
  8. 8.Your rest — recovered time is not the same as performed leisure.
Optimization is a posture, not a goal. Some things resist it for good reasons.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-96
Ember

Nine Signs You Have Outgrown This Season

  1. 1.The language of this chapter no longer fits in your mouth.
  2. 2.The people who organized this season feel like a previous translation.
  3. 3.The complaints you have rehearsed for years no longer move you.
  4. 4.The ambitions that named this season have either landed or stopped mattering.
  5. 5.The rooms of your life feel slightly off, like they were furnished for a previous you.
  6. 6.Your body is bored in ways your mind has not yet acknowledged.
  7. 7.The next version of you is already speaking, quietly, when nobody is listening.
  8. 8.You feel grateful and finished at the same time, without contradiction.
  9. 9.You begin to resent the rituals that used to feel like home.
Outgrowing is gradual. The departure is sudden.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-97
Midnight

Eight Questions To Ask Yourself Before Forty

  1. 1.What did you give up believing you could not have, and was the verdict actually true?
  2. 2.Which relationships in your life are decades old by inertia, not by choice?
  3. 3.What part of your current life would your twenty-year-old self refuse to recognize?
  4. 4.Which grievances have you been carrying as identity rather than as data?
  5. 5.What would you do this year if you knew the runway was twenty good years and then changed terms?
  6. 6.Whose approval are you still organizing around, and is it the same person as twenty years ago?
  7. 7.What have you been postponing on the assumption of an indefinite future?
  8. 8.What part of yourself have you amputated to fit your current life?
The audit at forty is more painful when postponed. Run it earlier.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-98
Tobacco

Nine Patterns You Are Ending In Your Family Line

  1. 1.The silence about money that produced anxiety in three generations.
  2. 2.The contempt dressed as humor at family gatherings.
  3. 3.The secret-keeping that protected the harm at the expense of the harmed.
  4. 4.The favoritism between siblings that calcified into adult roles.
  5. 5.The emotional outsourcing to one child who could not refuse.
  6. 6.The use of religion to bypass therapy and other accountability.
  7. 7.The passive-aggressive communication style that pretended to be politeness.
  8. 8.The withholding of love as a tool of control.
  9. 9.The story that any boundary against family is a betrayal of family.
The pattern survived for generations because no one cost themselves enough to end it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-99
Candle

Eight Signs You Are The First In Your Family To Do This Work

  1. 1.You are interrogated about therapy by family members who have never been.
  2. 2.You are accused of being cold for behaviors they would call respectful from a friend.
  3. 3.You are isolated in family WhatsApps when you decline to participate in the usual jokes.
  4. 4.You feel guilty about ordinary boundaries that strangers would consider unremarkable.
  5. 5.Your changes are framed as a betrayal of family identity.
  6. 6.Your partner is blamed for behaviors that pre-dated the relationship.
  7. 7.You are quietly the role model for cousins who do not yet have the vocabulary.
  8. 8.You feel lonely in a specific way: doing work no one in your family has ever done.
The first generation pays a particular price. Notice the shape of it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-LISTS-100
Ash

Nine Things Your Children Will Not Have To Do

  1. 1.Apologize for taking up space they have every right to take up.
  2. 2.Translate their interior into a language designed for someone else.
  3. 3.Suppress their feelings to manage your unprocessed ones.
  4. 4.Choose between loving you and being themselves.
  5. 5.Watch you punish them for being too much.
  6. 6.Earn love they were entitled to from the beginning.
  7. 7.Carry the silence you inherited and chose not to pass down.
  8. 8.Spend a decade in therapy decoding what was never said to them.
  9. 9.Become the parent they needed because you could not be.
The work you do quietly today is invisible savings for them. Track it anyway.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone

3 Points · Inkwell

50 cards · 9 BG presets
IMP-3POINTS-201
Ink

Three Reasons People Tolerate Cruelty From Family They Would Not From Strangers

  1. 1.The cruelty arrived early and got coded as normal. Children calibrate to whatever they get. The treatment becomes the baseline. Decades later, the same treatment feels like home, even when the body recognizes it as harm.
  2. 2.Leaving family is socially expensive in a way leaving strangers is not. The social cost is real. Family estrangement carries stigma that other endings do not. Many people pay decades of small costs to avoid the one larger one.
  3. 3.Some families perform love loudly. Confusion is the goal. The performance creates uncertainty about whether the harm was real. The uncertainty is the engineering. Stranger cruelty is rarely accompanied by I love you in the same breath.
Familiarity is not love. It is just familiarity.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-3POINTS-202
Slate

Three Signs Anger Is Actually Fear

  1. 1.The anger arrived faster than the situation warranted. Disproportionate anger is rarely about the current trigger. The speed of escalation is the signal. The body recognized something familiar and called the closer feeling.
  2. 2.The anger evaporates when the threat clarifies. If the source resolves and the anger does not, it was anger. If the resolution dissolves the anger immediately, it was fear with a louder voice.
  3. 3.You feel a kind of shame after, with no clear cause. Post-rage shame about your own intensity is data. The shame is about having shown the fear, dressed up. The dress did not fool you, only the audience.
Anger is the legible cover for the illegible feeling underneath.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-3POINTS-203
Tide

Three Ways Self-Sabotage Looks Like Discipline

  1. 1.Punishing routines that do not actually serve a goal. Strict regimens that exhaust without producing outcomes are not discipline. They are self-punishment with better PR. The diagnostic is whether the routine is sustainable and improving things, or whether it is a daily atonement.
  2. 2.Refusing rest you have already earned. Earned rest is part of high performance. Refusing it as weakness is the saboteur disguised as the coach. The output decays. The self-image holds.
  3. 3.Setting bars no one could clear, then collapsing under them. The bar is set high enough to guarantee failure. The collapse confirms the original story about not being enough. The cycle is the engineering, not the failure.
The vocabulary of virtue often hides the behavior of harm.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-3POINTS-204
Abyss

Three Reasons You Keep Choosing The Same Wrong Person

  1. 1.They recreate a familiar emotional weather. What feels like chemistry is often pattern recognition. The nervous system identifies the temperature of childhood and reports it as compatibility.
  2. 2.Their inconsistency activates a known game. If you grew up with unpredictable affection, consistent love feels boring. The activation system mistakes turbulence for aliveness.
  3. 3.You are completing an old conversation, not starting a new one. Each new partnership is partly an attempt to win the argument that the original audience refused to have. The current person is rarely the one the argument was meant for.
The pattern is older than the person. The person is recognizable, not chosen.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-3POINTS-205
Storm

Three Patterns That Look Like Love And Are Not

  1. 1.The intense feeling that arrives during reconciliation. Make-up euphoria is not love. It is the nervous system's relief at the threat passing. The intensity reflects the size of the fear, not the depth of the connection.
  2. 2.The compulsion to check on them when you have been ignored. Compulsive checking is not affection. It is the brain trying to resolve unbearable uncertainty. The feeling is real. The interpretation is the error.
