  5. 5.Refuse - To over-explain. A therapist who needs reasons may not have been the right therapist.
  6. 6.Plan - The next step before ending. Therapeutic gap usually undoes recent progress.
Therapist fit is a real variable. The wrong fit costs years.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-148
Velvet

How to Find A New Therapist

  1. 1.List - Your specific needs. Specialty, modality, demographics, logistics. Vague searches produce vague matches.
  2. 2.Ask - Three people you trust for referrals. The trusted-source channel often outperforms directory searches.
  3. 3.Screen - Three to five candidates in fifteen-minute calls. Most therapists offer these.
  4. 4.Test - Three sessions before deciding. The first session is rarely diagnostic.
  5. 5.Notice - Your body during sessions. Activation is normal. Numbness or dread is data.
  6. 6.Refuse - To stay out of inertia. Continuing with a misfit therapist out of guilt costs the work itself.
Finding the right therapist is a search problem. Approach it like one.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-149
Bone

How to Take Medication Seriously Without Identifying With It

  1. 1.Take - It as prescribed, every day, including the days you feel fine. The fine days are partly the medication.
  2. 2.Track - Side effects for the first six weeks. Most resolve. Some indicate the wrong dose or wrong drug.
  3. 3.Refuse - To stop without consulting the prescriber. Even tapering down requires structure.
  4. 4.Separate - The medication from your identity. I am on X is fine. I am a person on X is more accurate.
  5. 5.Update - Your prescriber on changes — life, drugs, supplements. Interactions are real.
  6. 6.Plan - Reviews at six and twelve months. Medication is rarely a forever decision made on the first day.
The medication is a tool. The tool is not the toolbox is not the self.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-150
Sand

How to Tell A Partner You Are Not Okay

  1. 1.Pick - A calm moment. Not in the middle of their hard day.
  2. 2.State - Plainly. I am not okay right now and I want you to know. No long preamble.
  3. 3.Specify - What you want from telling them. To be heard, helped, given space. Naming reduces guessing.
  4. 4.Refuse - To minimize once you start. It's nothing big undoes the disclosure.
  5. 5.Allow - Their reaction without managing it. Their first response may be imperfect. Wait for the second.
  6. 6.Return - To the conversation in days. One conversation rarely completes the disclosure.
The honesty is the relationship. The performance of being fine is the wedge.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-151
Forge

How to Tell Someone Their Behavior Is Hurting You

  1. 1.Choose - A calm time, not the moment of the hurt. Hot delivery rarely produces hearing.
  2. 2.Name - The specific behavior. When you interrupt me in meetings is workable. You disrespect me is not.
  3. 3.State - The impact in one sentence. It makes me feel small in front of clients.
  4. 4.Ask - For specific change. I am asking you to stop doing that. Concrete asks are easier to honor.
  5. 5.Refuse - To debate whether the behavior was hurtful. Their interpretation is not the topic.
  6. 6.Watch - What happens in the next thirty days. The response to the request is the data, not the response in the moment.
The conversation is harder if you postpone it. The cost compounds with each delay.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-152
Anvil

How to Set A Financial Boundary

  1. 1.State - The boundary as a rule, not as a feeling. I do not loan to family is clearer than I am uncomfortable.
  2. 2.Refuse - To negotiate the amount when the principle is no. Negotiating the amount accepts the premise.
  3. 3.Document - Joint financial agreements in writing. Verbal agreements about money do not survive contact with stress.
  4. 4.Separate - Accounts you can. Shared accounts are fine when chosen, expensive when defaulted into.
  5. 5.Discuss - Money quarterly with partners. Quarterly is rare enough to feel manageable and frequent enough to prevent drift.
  6. 6.Hold - Through requests that frame the boundary as cold. Money clarity is the warmest long-term position.
Money is a vocabulary. The vocabulary needs explicit, written, mutually-agreed terms.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-153
Smithy

How to Set A Time Boundary

  1. 1.Audit - Where your hours go for two weeks. The leaks are where the boundary needs to land.
  2. 2.Decide - The non-negotiable blocks. Sleep, family meal, exercise, one focus block. Anchor the calendar around them first.
  3. 3.Refuse - Requests that violate the blocks. Most requests are not as urgent as they present.
  4. 4.Reduce - Open availability. Calendars that show free time invite meetings to fill it.
  5. 5.Name - The boundary when challenged. That block is non-negotiable. Suggest another time.
  6. 6.Hold - Through the first month. The system tests new boundaries before accepting them.
Time is the resource everyone treats as renewable. It is not.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-154
Furnace

How to Refuse An Invitation Without An Excuse

  1. 1.Decline - Briefly. Thanks for thinking of me. I am not going to make it.
  2. 2.Refuse - To explain. The explanation invites alternatives that defeat the refusal.
  3. 3.Resist - The fake calendar conflict. Lying creates work and damages trust if discovered.
  4. 4.Sit - With the brief social discomfort. The discomfort fades within hours.
  5. 5.Hold - Through the second ask. Most invitations get re-issued with adjusted terms.
  6. 6.Notice - Who responds well to a clean no. Those people are the keepers.
Excuses invite negotiation. *No* by itself does not.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-155
Cinder

How to End A Long Phone Call

  1. 1.Notice - The natural ending. Most calls have a clear closing moment that gets missed by both parties.
  2. 2.Name - The ending. It has been good to talk. I am going to let you go.
  3. 3.Refuse - The third one more thing. Each addendum trains a longer next call.
  4. 4.Stand - Up. Physical posture shift makes the ending easier to maintain.
  5. 5.Promise - Concretely. I will call you next week is better than we should talk soon.
  6. 6.Hang - Up warmly but firmly. The end is the most-remembered part of the call.
Most long calls are not actually long. They have ended and continued anyway.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-156
Dusk

How to Leave A Party Early

  1. 1.Decide - Your exit time before you arrive. I am leaving by ten is easier to hold than improvising.
  2. 2.Tell - The host quietly at arrival or mid-event. I have an early morning. I will slip out around ten.
  3. 3.Find - The host before leaving. Thank them briefly. Do not narrate why.
  4. 4.Refuse - The one more drink loop. It exists to keep the room full, not for your benefit.
  5. 5.Move - Decisively when you leave. Lingering goodbyes invite reabsorption.
  6. 6.Send - A thank-you the next day. The follow-up smooths the early exit.
The exit is a skill. Skilled exits leave goodwill and an open invitation next time.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-157
Iron

How to Decline A Request For Unpaid Work

  1. 1.Recognize - The ask. Could you take a look at and we'd love your input on are unpaid-work signals.
  2. 2.Decline - Briefly. I do not take on unpaid work at this stage of my career.
  3. 3.Offer - A paid version. My consultation rate is X. I am happy to schedule.
  4. 4.Refuse - Exposure as compensation. Exposure does not pay rent.
  5. 5.Hold - Through the disappointed response. The response is not your responsibility.
  6. 6.Note - Repeat askers. People who ask once often understand. People who ask repeatedly are testing.
Free is the most expensive price you can charge for your skills.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-158
Velvet

How to Say No Without Softening

  1. 1.Pre-decide - The no before the conversation. Negotiating with yourself in real time produces soft no.
  2. 2.Speak - Plainly. No, I cannot. Two words. Full sentence.
  3. 3.Refuse - To add justifications. Justifications invite counter-arguments.
  4. 4.Resist - The urge to fill silence after the no. The silence is the closing.
  5. 5.Repeat - If pressed. No, I cannot. Same words, same tone. Repetition is the lock.
  6. 6.Maintain - Warmth in tone. Warm no is the most sustainable version.
Soft no is heard as soft maybe. Hard no closes the conversation.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-159
Bone

How to Repeat A No Without Escalating

  1. 1.Repeat - The same no in the same words. Variation invites negotiation.
  2. 2.Refuse - To raise your voice. Volume implies the no is reactive. The no is settled.
  3. 3.Hold - Eye contact, neutral expression. Body language carries the no when the words have been heard.
  4. 4.Refuse - New arguments by them. Yes, and the answer is still no.
  5. 5.Exit - After three repetitions if they continue. Each beyond three trains them to ask more.
  6. 6.Wait - For the next interaction. They will test less if the no held this time.
The escalation only happens if you cooperate. Refuse the cooperation.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-160
Sand

How to Say No In Writing

  1. 1.Lead - With the no. I am not going to be able to take this on. No preamble.
  2. 2.Limit - The explanation to one sentence. Long written no invites long replies.
  3. 3.Refuse - Apologies that are not warranted. Sorry but I can't invites I really need this.
  4. 4.Close - Politely. Wishing you the best with the project. Short and final.
  5. 5.Send - Without re-drafting twenty times. The third draft is usually the best one.
  6. 6.Refuse - To respond to follow-up pressure beyond a final note. My answer holds. Best of luck.
Written no is the most enforceable no. It also leaves a record.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-161
Forge

How to Detox From Chronic Overworking

  1. 1.Stop - Adding new commitments. The first move is to stop digging.
  2. 2.Audit - Your current commitments. List them. Most lists shock the writer.
  3. 3.Cut - Three things this week. The smallest cuts produce the largest relief.
  4. 4.Sleep - Eight hours, not seven. The half-hour difference is the recovery.
  5. 5.Reintroduce - Boredom. The discomfort of unfilled time is the diagnostic.
  6. 6.Hold - Six weeks. Recovery is exponential. The first two weeks feel like nothing.
The body holds the receipts. The reset takes longer than the binge.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-162
Anvil

How to Take A Real Lunch Break

  1. 1.Leave - The desk. Eat anywhere else. The location change is the break.
  2. 2.Refuse - Email during. The inbox can hold for thirty minutes. It has held for longer.
  3. 3.Set - Thirty minutes minimum. Twenty is a snack. Thirty is a break.
  4. 4.Eat - Slowly. The slow eating is the rest, not just the food.
  5. 5.Walk - Five minutes after. Movement after a meal compounds the reset.
  6. 6.Return - Refreshed. If you return more tired, the break was not a break.
Lunch at the desk is not a lunch break. The body knows this.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-163
Smithy

How to Sleep When Stressed

  1. 1.Dim - All lights two hours before bed. The body reads brightness as morning.
  2. 2.Stop - Caffeine after noon. Slow metabolizers feel afternoon caffeine at midnight.
  3. 3.Move - Earlier in the day. Late workouts spike cortisol when you need it falling.
  4. 4.Write - Tomorrow's worry list on paper before bed. Externalizing reduces internal looping.
  5. 5.Refuse - Screens in bed. The light alone delays melatonin by an hour.
  6. 6.Hold - A consistent wake time, even after bad nights. The wake time anchors the next night.
Sleep is engineered, not willed. Run the engineering.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-164
Furnace

How to Eat Alone In Public Without Dread

  1. 1.Choose - A counter or bar seat for early attempts. The configuration normalizes solo dining.
  2. 2.Bring - Nothing. The book is a shield. The shield prolongs the discomfort.
  3. 3.Order - Confidently. Hesitation telegraphs unease that nobody else was projecting.
  4. 4.Eat - Slowly. Pretending to rush trains the dread.
  5. 5.Look - Around. Most people are not looking at you. Looking proves the asymmetry.
  6. 6.Return - The next week. Repeated exposure is the cure.
The dread is not about the meal. It is about being seen alone.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-165
Cinder

How to Stop Drinking For A Season

  1. 1.Decide - A length. Thirty days minimum. Open-ended is harder than time-bound.
  2. 2.Tell - Two people. The accountability is small but enough.
  3. 3.Replace - The ritual, not the substance. Pour seltzer in the same glass at the same time.
  4. 4.Refuse - The first few invitations that test you. The first month is the steepest.
  5. 5.Notice - Sleep, skin, energy, mood. The data accumulates faster than expected.
  6. 6.Decide - At the end. Some return to drinking with better information. Some do not return.
Sobriety is information, not a verdict. Run the experiment to see the data.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-166
Dusk

How to Take A Digital Sabbath

  1. 1.Pick - A day. The same day each week, anchored to existing structure.
  2. 2.Prepare - Saturday's logistics on Friday. The sabbath fails when the day requires the phone.
  3. 3.Power - Down. Airplane mode is permeable. Off is solid.
  4. 4.Plan - Analog activities. The unstructured day is harder than the unstructured hour.
  5. 5.Refuse - Just one check. The check is the failure mode.
  6. 6.Hold - Six weeks. The first few feel restless. By week six, it is the best day of the week.
One day off the feed reveals what the other six have been costing you.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-167
Iron

How to Walk Daily Without Making It A Project

  1. 1.Decide - A duration that is small. Twenty minutes, not sixty. Sixty becomes negotiable.
  2. 2.Anchor - It to existing routine. After morning coffee. After lunch. Anchored is durable.
  3. 3.Refuse - Apps, trackers, music for the first week. The walk is the goal.
  4. 4.Hold - Through the boredom. The boredom is data, not failure.
  5. 5.Resist - Making it productive. No podcasts at first. Quiet is the medicine.
  6. 6.Continue - On bad days. Especially on bad days. The walk is rarely the wrong call.
Walking is not exercise. Treating it as exercise ruins it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-168
Velvet

How to Notice Early Signs Of Burnout

  1. 1.Track - Sunday dread. Steady increase over four weeks is the early signal.
  2. 2.Notice - Cynicism about work you used to enjoy. The shift is rarely about the work.
  3. 3.Audit - Recovery. Weekends that no longer feel different from Mondays are data.
  4. 4.Watch - Sleep quality, not just sleep quantity. Bad sleep with full hours is mid-stage.
  5. 5.Measure - Patience with small requests. Disproportionate irritation is late-stage.
  6. 6.Act - On three signals. Waiting for four means recovery takes months, not weeks.
Burnout has a long runway. The signs arrive in order. Catch them early.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-169
Bone

How to Recover From A Bad Night Of Sleep

  1. 1.Refuse - Extra caffeine. The crash multiplies the original deficit.
  2. 2.Eat - Protein early. The brain runs better on food than on stimulants.
  3. 3.Walk - Outside for ten minutes. Morning light anchors the next night's sleep.
  4. 4.Reduce - The day's load if possible. One hard meeting is plenty for a bad-night day.
  5. 5.Resist - Long naps. Twenty minutes maximum. Long naps wreck the next night too.
  6. 6.Hold - A regular bedtime that night. The recovery is one good night, not weekend catch-up.
One bad night is not a crisis. Most people compound it into one.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-170
Sand

How to Age Without Performing Youth

  1. 1.Notice - The performance. The slang you adopt. The references you fake. The aesthetic you maintain.
  2. 2.Refuse - The treatments that hide rather than maintain. Hiding is expensive and unending.
  3. 3.Wear - Clothes that fit the body you have now, not the body of ten years ago.
  4. 4.Speak - In your own register. Your decade has its own vocabulary. Use it.
  5. 5.Allow - Knowledge to show. Decades produce earned authority that performance cannot fake.
  6. 6.Befriend - People at multiple ages. Single-age cohorts compound the dread.
The performance is exhausting. The drop is permission, not loss.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-171
Forge

How to Give Bad News

  1. 1.Lead - With the headline. I have hard news. The recipient prepares for hard.
  2. 2.State - The news in one sentence. The project is canceled. We are letting go of the team next month.
  3. 3.Wait - Through the silence. The first reaction is rarely the considered one.
  4. 4.Refuse - To soften the news into ambiguity. Ambiguity prolongs the wound.
  5. 5.Offer - What comes next. Concrete next steps reduce the freefall.
  6. 6.Stay - Available. The first conversation is rarely the only one needed.
The way you give it is half of what is heard. Run the form.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-172
Anvil

How to Start A Hard Conversation

  1. 1.Choose - A calm, private moment. Hard conversations in public spaces become performances.
  2. 2.Name - The topic up front. I want to talk about something hard. Naming reduces ambient anxiety.
  3. 3.State - The reason. Because the current pattern is not sustainable for me.
  4. 4.Refuse - Sarcasm, jokes, or hedging. The opening hedge undermines the rest.
  5. 5.Listen - First, even if you brought the topic. Their response shapes what comes next.
  6. 6.Plan - One concrete ask. Hard conversations without asks are venting, not conversation.
The opening sentence sets the temperature for the whole conversation.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-173
Smithy

How to Repair A Friendship Gone Quiet

  1. 1.Reach - Out without justifying the gap. Thinking of you. Want to catch up? Short and warm.
  2. 2.Wait - For their response without re-sending. The wait is information.
  3. 3.Plan - Specifically when they respond. Tuesday at six? Concrete proposals get accepted.
  4. 4.Show - Up fully. The first reconnect is not the place to discuss why it went quiet.
  5. 5.Refuse - To audit the friendship in the first conversation. Audit later, alone.
  6. 6.Decide - After three attempts. If they consistently match your effort, the friendship is salvageable. If not, you have your answer.
The quiet has a cause. Sometimes the cause is logistics. Sometimes it is the friendship.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-174
Furnace

How to Apologize Publicly Without Grovelling

  1. 1.Acknowledge - The specific harm, not a vague I am sorry if I hurt anyone.
  2. 2.Take - Responsibility without blaming context. I made a mistake — full stop.
  3. 3.State - What you will do differently. Concrete is the difference between apology and PR.
  4. 4.Refuse - To attack the people who pointed it out. The attack erases the apology.
  5. 5.Limit - The post. One acknowledgment is enough. Three is the grovel.
  6. 6.Hold - The follow-through privately. The visible part is the smaller part of the repair.
The grovel reads as performance. The honest acknowledgment reads as character.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-175
Cinder

How to Refuse To Apologize For Something You Stand By

  1. 1.Decide - Privately whether the action was right. The decision is the foundation.
  2. 2.Refuse - To apologize for the act. I do not regret what I said.
  3. 3.Acknowledge - Any harm caused while standing by the act. I see this is painful. I would still say it again.
  4. 4.Hold - Through pressure from people who want a public retraction. Pressure is not evidence.
  5. 5.Resist - Hollow phrases that look like apologies. The fake apology costs more than no apology.
  6. 6.Wait - For the second wave. Most controversies fade in a week. Standing produces respect over time.
Apologizing for the right thing is a betrayal of yourself. The cost compounds.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-176
Dusk

How to Make Small Talk You Do Not Dread

  1. 1.Ask - About the day, not the life. Have you had a good week? opens. What do you do? closes.
  2. 2.Listen - For the second sentence. The first sentence is rehearsed. The second is honest.
  3. 3.Refuse - To compete on stories. Matching small talk is fine. Topping is not.
  4. 4.Specify - Your follow-ups. What was the best part of the trip? beats How was it?
  5. 5.Offer - One personal detail. Reciprocity is the engine.
  6. 6.Exit - Cleanly. It was good to meet you is enough.
Small talk is not stupid. It is the protocol for not-yet-trusted strangers.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-177
Iron

How to Leave A Conversation Gracefully When Bored

  1. 1.Notice - The boredom early. The early exit is easier than the late one.
  2. 2.Refuse - To fake interest. The fake interest prolongs everyone's suffering.
  3. 3.Use - A clean exit phrase. It has been good to chat. I am going to grab another drink.
  4. 4.Move - Decisively. Lingering reopens the conversation.
  5. 5.Refuse - Long goodbyes. Short and warm beats long and exhausted.
  6. 6.Hold - No grudge. Boredom is not their fault. It is incompatibility, not failure.
Boredom is data. The graceful exit honors both parties.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-178
Velvet

How to Remember Names

  1. 1.Listen - When the name is spoken. Most names are missed at the moment of introduction.
  2. 2.Repeat - It immediately. Good to meet you, Sarah. The repetition is the encoding.
  3. 3.Use - It twice in the conversation. Twice is enough to install.
  4. 4.Associate - Visually. The face plus the name as one packet.
  5. 5.Write - It down within an hour. Phone notes are not cheating.
  6. 6.Refuse - To pretend. I am sorry, please remind me of your name is warmer than fake recognition.
Remembering names is a courtesy. Most failures are not memory — they are attention.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-179
Bone

How to Ask Better Questions

  1. 1.Specify - Open-ended questions over closed ones. How did that go? beats Did it go well?
  2. 2.Follow - Up on details, not on summaries. What did you do next? extracts more than interesting.
  3. 3.Refuse - Two-part questions. Pick one. The other can wait.
  4. 4.Wait - For the answer before asking the next one. Premature follow-up signals you were not listening.
  5. 5.Ask - About process, not just outcome. How did you decide? opens more than What did you decide?
  6. 6.Resist - Leading questions. Don't you think invites agreement, not honesty.
The quality of the question sets the ceiling of the answer.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-180
Sand

How to Listen Without Preparing Your Response

  1. 1.Notice - When your mind is composing. The catch is the work.
  2. 2.Return - To their words mid-thought. Wait, what did they just say? is the reset.
  3. 3.Ask - Follow-ups that prove you heard. Specificity confirms attention.
  4. 4.Refuse - The urge to match every story with one of yours. Their story can stand alone.
  5. 5.Sit - Briefly with silence after they finish. The silence is part of the listening.
  6. 6.Respond - From what was said, not from what you had prepared. Prepared responses leak.
Listening is the rarest social skill because it requires the suspension of self.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-181
Forge

How to Look At Your Bank Account When Scared

  1. 1.Decide - A time. Pick a calm hour, not one stolen between obligations.
  2. 2.Sit - Down with paper, pen, and the actual numbers. Write what is there.
  3. 3.Refuse - To extrapolate to disasters. List the actual balance, the actual income, the actual bills.
  4. 4.Notice - The fear is rarely about the number. It is usually about a story attached to the number.
  5. 5.Plan - Three small actions. Not a complete overhaul. Three reduces overwhelm.
  6. 6.Schedule - The next look. Weekly. The frequency is the cure for the fear.
The fear is heavier than the math. Look at the math anyway.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-182
Anvil

How to Ask Someone What They Earn

  1. 1.Pick - The right person. Same role, similar tenure, trusted. Asking your boss is different.
  2. 2.Frame - It plainly. I am trying to calibrate. Would you mind sharing your range?
  3. 3.Refuse - To soften with apologies. The ask is professional.
  4. 4.Share - Your number first if helpful. Reciprocity opens the disclosure.
  5. 5.Respect - A no. Some people are uncomfortable. The asking does no harm if respected.
  6. 6.Compile - What you learn. Three data points beats one anecdote.
The taboo serves the people who pay you less than you deserve.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-183
Smithy

How to Refuse To Lend Money

  1. 1.Decide - Before the request. I do not loan money as a rule, not a feeling.
  2. 2.Decline - Briefly. I do not loan money. It is a rule I hold for everyone.
  3. 3.Refuse - To adjust the amount. The rule is the rule, not a starting bid.
  4. 4.Offer - A one-time gift if you can. A gift has a different relationship to the friendship than a loan.
  5. 5.Suggest - Alternatives. Financial counseling, payment plans with creditors, a job referral.
  6. 6.Hold - Through the cold spell. Most relationships survive a clean refusal better than a loan that goes unpaid.
The loan to a loved one usually costs both the money and the relationship.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-184
Furnace

How to Handle A Financial Windfall

  1. 1.Wait - Sixty days before spending any of it. The wait separates impulse from intention.
  2. 2.Pay - Down high-interest debt first. The return is guaranteed and large.
  3. 3.Save - Six months of expenses if you do not have it. The cushion is worth more than any single purchase.
  4. 4.Invest - A portion in long-term, boring instruments. Boring is the strategy.
  5. 5.Allow - Ten percent for joy. The reward integrates the experience.
  6. 6.Refuse - To tell most people. The advice and requests that arrive are usually expensive.
Windfalls disappear faster than earned money. Engineering is required.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-185
Cinder

How to Budget Without Shame

  1. 1.Track - Spending for one month without judgment. The data is the goal.
  2. 2.Sort - Spending into three categories. Survival, joy, and avoidable. Each category has different rules.
  3. 3.Refuse - Strict austerity. Strict budgets fail like strict diets.
  4. 4.Allow - Joy spending intentionally. Unplanned joy spending becomes guilt spending.
  5. 5.Adjust - Monthly, not weekly. The budget is a rolling instrument.
  6. 6.Notice - The patterns that arise. Pattern is more useful than judgment.
The budget is a tool. Shame turns the tool into a punishment device.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-186
Dusk

How to Plan Retirement When Behind

  1. 1.Calculate - Your number. Not perfectly. Roughly. Round numbers reveal the shape.
  2. 2.Increase - Contributions by one percent per quarter. Small increases compound.
  3. 3.Use - The catch-up provisions if over fifty. They exist for this reason.
  4. 4.Reduce - Lifestyle inflation. Each avoided upgrade compounds into the retirement.
  5. 5.Refuse - Risky catch-up strategies. Late-stage gambles produce late-stage disasters.
  6. 6.Plan - Continued work past traditional retirement. Health and meaning are arguments for, not just income.
Late is not too late. The math is still in your favor if you start now.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-187
Iron

How to Talk To A Partner About Debt

  1. 1.Schedule - A money conversation outside of normal life. Pick a Saturday morning.
  2. 2.Disclose - Numbers first. Here is what I have before here is what I have done.
  3. 3.Refuse - To moralize the debt. Most debt has structural causes. Address the structure.
  4. 4.Plan - Together. Joint plans for joint households reduce the secrecy that compounds the problem.
  5. 5.Track - Progress monthly. The visibility is the structural change.
  6. 6.Celebrate - Small milestones. Long debt journeys without acknowledgment usually fail.
Debt is a vocabulary. The conversation is overdue if you have not had it.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-188
Velvet

How to Negotiate A Salary

  1. 1.Research - Market rate before you negotiate. Bring data, not feelings.
  2. 2.Wait - For the offer before naming a number. First-named numbers are usually the wrong ones.
  3. 3.State - A number ten to twenty percent above your target. Anchor high to land at target.
  4. 4.Refuse - The first counter. Almost every first counter has more in it.
  5. 5.Ask - About more than salary. Equity, signing bonus, PTO, remote flexibility — each is negotiable.
  6. 6.Get - It in writing. Verbal promises about future raises are not enforceable.
The negotiation is part of the job. Skipping it makes the rest of the job worse.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-189
Bone

How to Invest Without Expertise

  1. 1.Open - A brokerage account. The friction of opening one is the only barrier most people cannot get past.
  2. 2.Choose - Low-cost index funds. The simplicity is the strategy.
  3. 3.Automate - Monthly contributions. The automation is the discipline.
  4. 4.Refuse - To check daily. The volatility is noise, not signal.
  5. 5.Continue - Through downturns. The discipline is buying when others are scared.
  6. 6.Wait - Ten years. The math requires the time. Anything shorter is gambling, not investing.
Investing badly is expensive. Investing simply is not.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-190
Sand

How to Set Up Finances After A Breakup

  1. 1.Document - What is yours, what is theirs, what is joint. Documentation is the foundation.
  2. 2.Separate - Joint accounts before the conversation ends. Delay produces friction.
  3. 3.Change - Passwords, beneficiaries, account access. Each is a small but critical step.
  4. 4.Refuse - Verbal agreements about money. Written or it did not happen.
  5. 5.Rebuild - Solo budgets. Single-income math is different. Treat it as new, not as half-old.
  6. 6.Wait - Six months before major financial decisions. Grief and money are a bad pairing.
The separation is logistics. The logistics are time-sensitive.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-191
Forge

How to Do An Annual Life Audit

  1. 1.Schedule - A specific day. The same week each year. Anchored is durable.
  2. 2.Write - What worked this year and what did not. Specifically, not generally.
  3. 3.Audit - Relationships, work, body, money, attention. Each on its own page.
  4. 4.Refuse - To skim past hard data. The data is the point.
  5. 5.Decide - Three changes for the year ahead. Three is enough. Ten dilutes.
  6. 6.Set - Quarterly check-ins. Annual without quarterly degrades into nostalgia.
The audit is the leverage point. Most people skip it and wonder why nothing changes.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-192
Anvil

How to Decide Whether To Move

  1. 1.Audit - What about the current city is no longer working. List concretely.
  2. 2.Test - The new city for at least a week, in winter. Vacation visits are not data.
  3. 3.Notice - Whether you are running toward or away. Both are valid. Be honest about which.
  4. 4.Calculate - Costs — financial, social, professional. Most moves cost more than projected.
  5. 5.Refuse - To overweight the move's solving power. The interior moves with you.
  6. 6.Decide - On the move and the timeline separately. Decision is one variable. Date is another.
The decision is rarely about the new city. It is about leaving the current one.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-193
Smithy

How to Leave A City You Have Outgrown

  1. 1.Confirm - The outgrowing. Three consecutive years of plateau, not one bad year.
  2. 2.Tell - The closest people early. They will need time to adjust their model of you.
  3. 3.Plan - The exit at six months minimum. Quick moves leave loose ends that pull you back.
  4. 4.Refuse - To bad-mouth the city. The city served a season. Honor what it gave you.
  5. 5.Keep - The relationships that earned it. Geography changes. Real friendships survive.
  6. 6.Resist - The urge to return for the wedding, the funeral, the reunion. Returns calibrate your readiness.
The outgrowing is real. The leaving is a separate skill.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-194
Furnace

How to Start Over At Forty

  1. 1.Refuse - The frame of failure. The previous chapters trained skills you do not yet see.
  2. 2.Inventory - What transfers. Most skills are more portable than they look from inside the previous role.
  3. 3.Choose - One change to lead with. Career, geography, relationship. One at a time.
  4. 4.Network - Beyond your previous industry. The new chapter often starts with new rooms.
  5. 5.Accept - Slowness. The first year is investment. The second is build. The third is harvest.
  6. 6.Hold - Through the doubt month, which arrives around month nine. The doubt is part of the build.
Starting over is not starting fresh. The previous decades are the foundation, not the failure.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-195
Cinder

How to Make New Friends As An Adult

  1. 1.Choose - A recurring activity. Friendship needs repeated proximity, not chemistry.
  2. 2.Show - Up consistently. The first three months are attendance, not connection.
  3. 3.Initiate - First. Most adult friendships die from mutual waiting.
  4. 4.Refuse - To wait for chemistry. Real adult friendship grows slowly, then suddenly.
  5. 5.Invest - Specifically. Remember birthdays, follow up on hard weeks, show up at small events.
  6. 6.Accept - Most attempts will not become friendships. The success rate is low. Try more attempts.
Adult friendship requires deliberateness. Spontaneity stops working past thirty.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-196
Dusk

How to Live Alone Gracefully

  1. 1.Cook - Real meals for yourself. The act of feeding yourself well is the foundation.
  2. 2.Use - The whole apartment. Eating at the table. Bathing without rushing. Sitting in the chair you like.
  3. 3.Refuse - To treat solo time as a waiting room. The solo years are the actual life, not the prelude.
  4. 4.Invite - People over. Hosting solo is a skill. Practice.
  5. 5.Sleep - On your own schedule. The freedom from coordination is one of the gifts.
  6. 6.Notice - Your own rhythms. The data about yourself is the curriculum.
Living alone is not failure of partnership. It is a curriculum unavailable while partnered.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-197
Iron

How to Age Gracefully

  1. 1.Refuse - To compete with twenty-year-olds at twenty-year-old games.
  2. 2.Maintain - What works. Exercise, sleep, water, reading, walking. The basics compound.
  3. 3.Invest - In friendships across ages. Single-age cohorts age worse.
  4. 4.Allow - The body to change. Resistance to change accelerates the change.
  5. 5.Update - Your aesthetic without performing youth. The decade has its own register.
  6. 6.Speak - About your age plainly. The whisper makes the audience uncomfortable.
Grace is a posture. Most of the work is what you refuse to do, not what you do.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-198
Velvet

How to Face Mortality Directly

  1. 1.Sit - With the fact briefly each year. Not morbidly. Honestly.
  2. 2.Write - Your will. Update it every five years. The writing is the facing.
  3. 3.Tell - The people you love that you love them, in plain language, this year.
  4. 4.Refuse - The cultural denial. Most of the denial is what produces the panic.
  5. 5.Plan - Practical end-of-life choices. Medical directives. Funeral preferences. The clarity is a gift to those left behind.
  6. 6.Live - Now from the awareness, not anxiously but directly. The directness is the freedom.
The facing is the freedom. Most adults postpone it into anxiety.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-199
Bone

How to Choose A Partner Deliberately

  1. 1.Decide - Privately what you actually need. Not what looks attractive — what produces durable contentment.
  2. 2.Refuse - The chemistry-as-evidence frame. Chemistry is necessary but does not predict longevity.
  3. 3.Test - Their behavior in low-glamour conditions. Sick weeks, family events, financial pressure.
  4. 4.Notice - How they treat people they do not have to impress. The treatment of strangers is the future treatment of you.
  5. 5.Wait - For the second six months. The first six months is rehearsed. The second is them.
  6. 6.Choose - Capacity for repair over capacity for romance. Romance varies. Repair is the load-bearing wall.
Most partners are accidents of proximity. The deliberate choice is rare and worth the effort.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone
IMP-HOWTO-200
Sand

How to Leave Well

  1. 1.Decide - Privately before the conversation. Arriving open to negotiation produces ambiguous endings.
  2. 2.State - The decision clearly. I am leaving. No long preamble. No half-doors.
  3. 3.Acknowledge - What was good. The gratitude is real and worth voicing.
  4. 4.Refuse - To replay the grievances. The leaving is the answer; the grievance list is unnecessary.
  5. 5.Finish - The logistics. Loose ends call you back.
  6. 6.Stay - Gone after you go. Returns invalidate the leaving.
  7. 7.Allow - Grief on the other side. Leaving well includes mourning what you left.
Endings deserve craft. How you leave is what people remember.
MWMarcus Wise
Reels Safe Zone

    Import Cards

    Paste markdown with one card per block. Separate cards with a line containing only ---. Required keys at top: category, title (optional for question / quotes / maxims). Blank line, then body content parsed by category.

    Manage Imports

    You have 250 imported cards. Merge writes a new HTML file with library + imports baked in. Clear removes all imports from this file's storage.
